document.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Reviewing and Processing Evidence Guide

root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Reviewing and Processing Evidence Guide
root.summary: Introductory front matter and chapter 1 of the Cúram 8.1.2 Reviewing and Processing Evidence Guide. It explains how caseworkers review and maintain evidence using the main evidence lists (person tab, application, evidence type, active, in-edit, incoming, verifications, issues), dashboard and evidence object views, and actions such as create, modify, delete/discard, validate, and apply changes. It also covers evidence sharing/compare-and-resolve, parent/child evidence display, and when verifications or issues are available. The section includes detailed guidance for Multiple Participant Evidence Update for dynamic evidence, including supported page behavior, constraints (not compatible with parent-child evidence or Show All Fields), and performance considerations. The remaining pages are notices, privacy policy, and trademarks, with legal terms, warranty disclaimers, export compliance, and copyright information.

children.title: Note
children.summary: Introductory note for the document. It tells readers to review the Notices section on page 21 before using the information or the product it supports. Includes the Merative US L.P. copyright notice for 2012 and 2024.

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Cúram edition info for 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2 plus the start of “Reviewing and processing evidence.” Covers evidence lists (person tab, application, type, active, in-edit, incoming, verifications, issues), evidence views (dashboard, evidence object, view evidence), and evidence maintenance basics. Begins detailed guidance on creating and modifying evidence, including end-dating previous evidence, preferred indicators, validation, in-edit vs active records, corrections vs changes in circumstance, and related approval/activation behavior.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-20
children.summary: Covers evidence maintenance in Cúram: correcting, deleting, discarding, validating, and applying evidence changes, including approval, reassessment, cancellation, attribution periods, and CER differences. Also explains a hook point for automatic application of evidence changes. The latter half details the Multiple Participant Evidence Update for dynamic evidence: how create/modify/discard pages can apply changes to multiple case participants, plus suitability rules and constraints (not for static or parent-child evidence, mutually exclusive with “Show All Fields,” and performance considerations).

Cúram 8.1–8.1.2 evidence processing overview: evidence lists and views (person/application/type, active, in-edit, incoming, verifications, issues; dashboard, evidence object) plus evidence maintenance. Covers creating, modifying, correcting, deleting/discarding, validating, and applying evidence changes, including approval, reassessment, cancellation, attribution periods, CER differences, and a hook to auto-apply changes. Also explains Multiple Participant Evidence Update for dynamic evidence: apply create/modify/discard to multiple case participants, with constraints (not static or parent-child, mutually exclusive with Show All Fields, and performance-sensitive).

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: This section provides legal notices: sample data and fictitious names disclaimer, copyright/license terms for sample programs, and warranty/liability limitations. It includes the Merative privacy policy URL and a list of trademarks and ownership notices for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX, plus copyright years 2012 and 2024.

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: This section provides legal notices, including fictitious-name disclaimer, sample code copyright/license terms, privacy policy link, and trademark attributions. It lists Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle, Java, Linux, Microsoft, Windows, and UNIX trademarks, and notes other names may belong to third parties.

Legal notices for use of the publication: personal and enterprise reproduction is allowed only if proprietary notices are preserved, but distribution, display, derivative works, and external reuse require Merative consent. It includes no additional rights, export-law compliance, and Merative’s right to withdraw permission. The section also states the content is provided “as-is” with no warranties, notes no patent license is granted, disclaims responsibility for non-Merative products and third-party websites, and gives copyright © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: Table of contents for the document. Lists front matter and Chapter 1, “Reviewing and processing evidence,” with subsections on evidence lists, evidence views, and maintaining evidence (new, modify, remove active, discard in-edit, validate, apply changes, and update for multiple participants). Also includes notices, privacy policy, and trademarks. This node is useful for navigating to the document’s major sections rather than containing substantive instructional content.

children.title: 1 Reviewing and processing evidence
1.1 Evidence lists
children.summary: Describes the evidence lists available in the evidence framework and what each is used for. Covers the person tab evidence list for person/prospect person evidence, the application evidence list for application-related evidence, and the evidence type list for managing a single evidence type. Notes that evidence lists show summary and history details, including effective period, updates, latest activity, change reason, date/time, and user. Also explains key actions like view, update, delete, discard, undo, and the handling of deleted evidence.

children.title: 1.2 Evidence views
children.summary: Describes evidence views available to caseworkers: active evidence list, in-edit evidence list, incoming evidence, verifications list, and issues list. Explains what each list shows and what actions are possible, including deleting, editing, resolving, comparing, and managing verifications/issues. Also covers dashboard view and evidence object view, including filtering, child evidence display, evidence links, dashboard groups, and how parent/child/grandchild relationships are shown.

children.title: 1.3 Maintaining evidence
New evidence
children.summary: Explains how caseworkers create new evidence records from the Person, Case, or Application evidence tabs, including parent-child evidence, validation, and the initial in-edit status. Describes optional end-dating of existing evidence when creating a new record, including when the feature is available and the wizard conditions that trigger it. Also covers preferred indicator behavior: only one preferred record per evidence type is allowed, and creating a new preferred record clears the existing one and creates an in-edit change.

children.title: Modifying evidence
children.summary: Explains how evidence can be modified in Cúram 8.1.2. Covers which person/prospect person and case evidence records are editable, validation and approval constraints, and the difference between active, in-edit, and automatically activated person-tab evidence. Describes two update types: evidence corrections, which supersede the active record for the same period, and changes in circumstance, which create a new record effective from a specific date and preserve historical timelines.

children.title: Removing active evidence
children.summary: Explains how to remove active evidence by marking it pending deletion, not immediately deleting it. Notes restrictions: it cannot already be pending update, and parent evidence with active child evidence cannot be marked for deletion. Deletion is finalized only after Apply changes, after which status becomes Canceled and the evidence no longer affects eligibility/entitlement. Also covers deleting person/prospect person evidence from the person tab and its reassessment impact. Briefly distinguishes discarding In-Edit evidence and validating evidence changes as a pretest.

children.title: Discarding In-Edit evidence
children.summary: This section explains evidence maintenance actions around removal and validation. It describes deleting active evidence by marking it pending deletion, notes restrictions (cannot delete if already pending update or if it is a parent of active child evidence), and says deletion is finalized only after Apply changes, when status becomes Canceled and evidence stops affecting eligibility/entitlement. It also covers discarding In-Edit evidence, which is simpler because In-Edit records are not used for eligibility, except approved-but-not-activated records cannot be discarded. Finally, it introduces validate evidence record changes as a pretest for applying changes.

children.title: Validating evidence changes
children.summary: This section explains the validate evidence record changes function, which pretests apply evidence changes for a single evidence type. It helps users detect and fix errors before applying changes, since apply changes can process multiple evidence types at once and be harder to troubleshoot.

children.title: Applying evidence changes
children.summary: Explains the “apply evidence changes” function for Cúram Rules: users can apply all or selected pending evidence changes, which triggers validation at case and evidence-type levels, checks for required supervisor approval, supersedes corrected active evidence, cancels evidence pending removal, and reassesses impacted cases for eligibility/entitlement. Notes differences from CER, where these changes are handled in the timeline. Also describes a hook point for automating application of evidence changes, including automatic activation and cancellation.

children.title: Updating evidence for multiple participants
Multiple participant evidence update guidelines
children.summary: Guidelines for enabling multiple participant evidence updates in dynamic evidence. It explains the discard page behavior for cases with multiple active participants and the “Apply Discard To Other Case Participants” multi-select list. Suitability rules include: all evidence attributes must be applicable across participants, parent-child evidence is unsupported, create/modify/discard pages must apply to all participants, participant selection must come from a predefined set, “Show All Fields” must be disabled, the evidence should concern one-person business only, static evidence is unsupported, and transaction/performance impacts must be considered.

Explains the Multiple Participant Evidence Update for dynamic evidence, which lets create, modify, and discard actions apply to additional active case participants. Covers page behavior for create/modify/discard, including multi-select lists and transaction of changes to all selected participants. Lists suitability rules: all attributes must apply across participants, no parent-child evidence, the feature must cover create/modify/discard pages, participant selection must use a predefined set, Show All Fields must be off, evidence must concern one person only, static evidence is unsupported, and performance/validation impacts must be considered.

Cúram evidence maintenance overview: create new evidence from Person/Case/Application tabs, including parent-child links, validation, in-edit status, optional end-dating of prior evidence, and preferred indicator rules. Covers modifying evidence: editable record types, approval/validation constraints, corrections vs. changes in circumstance, and reassessment impacts. Also explains removing active evidence by pending deletion, discarding in-edit evidence, validating evidence changes as a pretest, applying evidence changes, and multiple participant evidence updates for dynamic evidence with key suitability and performance limits.

Overview of evidence navigation and maintenance in Cúram. Explains evidence lists (person tab, application, evidence type, active, in-edit, incoming, verifications, issues) and what each supports: view, update, delete, discard, undo, compare, resolve, and manage verifications/issues. Describes dashboard and evidence object views, including grouping, filtering, child evidence, and parent/child/grandchild display. Covers creating, validating, modifying, end-dating, discarding, and applying evidence, plus multiple-participant evidence update rules, constraints, and performance considerations.
